{{Infobox ancient site | name = Kusumyō-Shigesada Kofun | native_name =楠名・重定古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Shigesada Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Shigesada Kofun sign | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kusumyō-Shigesada Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|19|57|N|130|47|40|E|region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ukiha, Fukuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th to 7th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|19|57|N|130|47|40|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kusumyō-Shigesada Kofun'''|楠名・重定古墳}} is a pair of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Asada neighborhood of the city of [[Ukiha, Fukuoka|Ukiha]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus pair was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1922.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/173205|title=楠名・重定古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kusumyō-Shigesada Kofun are part of the Asada Kofun Cluster, located at the tip of the [[alluvial fan]] on the south bank of the [[Chikugo River]], at the foot of Mt. Minou. This area has many [[decorated kofun]], including the [[Tsukahanazuka Kofun]].<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Kusumyō Kofun is a two-tiered {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳||}}-style circular tomb with a diameter of approximately 32 meters and a height of 6 meters. The [[burial chamber]] is a horizontal-entry cave-style stone chamber that opens to the south, with the front chamber larger than the back chamber, and is thought to have been built in the early 7th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Shigesada Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has total length of approximately 51 meters, with a posterior circular portion diameter of approximately 44 meters, and height of 8.5 meters. it is believed to have been built in the latter half of the 6th century. The anterior portion has largely been lost, but the total length at the time of construction is estimated to be about 80 meters.The interior is a horizontal-entry stone burial chamber that opens almost to the south, with a total length of 17 meters made of large [[andesite]] monoliths. It is connected to the outside by a six meter long corridor. Decorative murals are on the walls, and although they have faded now, they use two colors, red and green, to create geometric patterns such as concentric circular patterns and [[bracken fern|''warabi'' fern]] patterns, as well as archery patterns depicting fletching, wristguard and quivers. The pattern composition is different between the rear chamber and the front chamber. The [[Edo period]] ''[[kokugaku]]'' scholar [[Hirata Atsutane]] interpreted this pattern as an ancient script, which he named {{nihongo|''Tsukushi moji''|筑紫文字|}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Historical records indicate that a large amount of [[grave goods]] could be found in this tumulus, but all have been lost to robbery. The interior of the tumulus was used as an [[air raid shelter]] during [[World War II]] and was visited by large numbers of people during the post-war archeology boom, causing much damage to the preservations of the murals.
The tumulus is about a ten-minute walk from [[Ukiha Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kyūdai Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Kusumyō-Shigesada Kofun | native_name =楠名・重定古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Shigesada Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Shigesada Kofun sign | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kusumyō-Shigesada Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|19|57|N|130|47|40|E|region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ukiha, Fukuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th to 7th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|19|57|N|130|47|40|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kusumyō-Shigesada Kofun'''|楠名・重定古墳}} is a pair of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Asada neighborhood of the city of [[Ukiha, Fukuoka|Ukiha]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus pair was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1922.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/173205|title=楠名・重定古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kusumyō-Shigesada Kofun are part of the Asada Kofun Cluster, located at the tip of the [[alluvial fan]] on the south bank of the [[Chikugo River]], at the foot of Mt. Minou. This area has many [[decorated kofun]], including the [[Tsukahanazuka Kofun]].<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Kusumyō Kofun is a two-tiered {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳||}}-style circular tomb with a diameter of approximately 32 meters and a height of 6 meters. The [[burial chamber]] is a horizontal-entry cave-style stone chamber that opens to the south, with the front chamber larger than the back chamber, and is thought to have been built in the early 7th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Shigesada Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has total length of approximately 51 meters, with a posterior circular portion diameter of approximately 44 meters, and height of 8.5 meters. it is believed to have been built in the latter half of the 6th century. The anterior portion has largely been lost, but the total length at the time of construction is estimated to be about 80 meters.The interior is a horizontal-entry stone burial chamber that opens almost to the south, with a total length of 17 meters made of large [[andesite]] monoliths. It is connected to the outside by a six meter long corridor. Decorative murals are on the walls, and although they have faded now, they use two colors, red and green, to create geometric patterns such as concentric circular patterns and [[bracken fern|''warabi'' fern]] patterns, as well as archery patterns depicting fletching, wristguard and quivers. The pattern composition is different between the rear chamber and the front chamber. The [[Edo period]] ''[[kokugaku]]'' scholar [[Hirata Atsutane]] interpreted this pattern as an ancient script, which he named {{nihongo|''Tsukushi moji''|筑紫文字|}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Historical records indicate that a large amount of [[grave goods]] could be found in this tumulus, but all have been lost to robbery. The interior of the tumulus was used as an [[air raid shelter]] during [[World War II]] and was visited by large numbers of people during the post-war archeology boom, causing much damage to the preservations of the murals.
The tumulus is about a ten-minute walk from [[Ukiha Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kyūdai Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Kusumyō-Shigesada Kofun | native_name =楠名・重定古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Shigesada Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Shigesada Kofun sign | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kusumyō-Shigesada Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|19|57|N|130|47|40|E|region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ukiha, Fukuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th to 7th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|19|57|N|130|47|40|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kusumyō-Shigesada Kofun'''|楠名・重定古墳}} is a pair of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Asada neighborhood of the city of [[Ukiha, Fukuoka|Ukiha]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus pair was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1922.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/173205|title=楠名・重定古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kusumyō-Shigesada Kofun are part of the Asada Kofun Cluster, located at the tip of the [[alluvial fan]] on the south bank of the [[Chikugo River]], at the foot of Mt. Minou. This area has many [[decorated kofun]], including the [[Tsukahanazuka Kofun]].<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Kusumyō Kofun is a two-tiered {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳||}}-style circular tomb with a diameter of approximately 32 meters and a height of 6 meters. The [[burial chamber]] is a horizontal-entry cave-style stone chamber that opens to the south, with the front chamber larger than the back chamber, and is thought to have been built in the early 7th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Shigesada Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has total length of approximately 51 meters, with a posterior circular portion diameter of approximately 44 meters, and height of 8.5 meters. it is believed to have been built in the latter half of the 6th century. The anterior portion has largely been lost, but the total length at the time of construction is estimated to be about 80 meters.The interior is a horizontal-entry stone burial chamber that opens almost to the south, with a total length of 17 meters made of large [[andesite]] monoliths. It is connected to the outside by a six meter long corridor. Decorative murals are on the walls, and although they have faded now, they use two colors, red and green, to create geometric patterns such as concentric circular patterns and [[bracken fern|''warabi'' fern]] patterns, as well as archery patterns depicting fletching, wristguard and quivers. The pattern composition is different between the rear chamber and the front chamber. The [[Edo period]] ''[[kokugaku]]'' scholar [[Hirata Atsutane]] interpreted this pattern as an ancient script, which he named {{nihongo|''Tsukushi moji''|筑紫文字|}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Historical records indicate that a large amount of [[grave goods]] could be found in this tumulus, but all have been lost to robbery. The interior of the tumulus was used as an [[air raid shelter]] during [[World War II]] and was visited by large numbers of people during the post-war archeology boom, causing much damage to the preservations of the murals.
The tumulus is about a ten-minute walk from [[Ukiha Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kyūdai Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Kusumyō-Shigesada Kofun | native_name =楠名・重定古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Shigesada Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Shigesada Kofun sign | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kusumyō-Shigesada Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|19|57|N|130|47|40|E|region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ukiha, Fukuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th to 7th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|19|57|N|130|47|40|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kusumyō-Shigesada Kofun'''|楠名・重定古墳}} is a pair of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Asada neighborhood of the city of [[Ukiha, Fukuoka|Ukiha]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus pair was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1922.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/173205|title=楠名・重定古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kusumyō-Shigesada Kofun are part of the Asada Kofun Cluster, located at the tip of the [[alluvial fan]] on the south bank of the [[Chikugo River]], at the foot of Mt. Minou. This area has many [[decorated kofun]], including the [[Tsukahanazuka Kofun]].<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Kusumyō Kofun is a two-tiered {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳||}}-style circular tomb with a diameter of approximately 32 meters and a height of 6 meters. The [[burial chamber]] is a horizontal-entry cave-style stone chamber that opens to the south, with the front chamber larger than the back chamber, and is thought to have been built in the early 7th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Shigesada Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has total length of approximately 51 meters, with a posterior circular portion diameter of approximately 44 meters, and height of 8.5 meters. it is believed to have been built in the latter half of the 6th century. The anterior portion has largely been lost, but the total length at the time of construction is estimated to be about 80 meters.The interior is a horizontal-entry stone burial chamber that opens almost to the south, with a total length of 17 meters made of large [[andesite]] monoliths. It is connected to the outside by a six meter long corridor. Decorative murals are on the walls, and although they have faded now, they use two colors, red and green, to create geometric patterns such as concentric circular patterns and [[bracken fern|''warabi'' fern]] patterns, as well as archery patterns depicting fletching, wristguard and quivers. The pattern composition is different between the rear chamber and the front chamber. The [[Edo period]] ''[[kokugaku]]'' scholar [[Hirata Atsutane]] interpreted this pattern as an ancient script, which he named {{nihongo|''Tsukushi moji''|筑紫文字|}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Historical records indicate that a large amount of [[grave goods]] could be found in this tumulus, but all have been lost to robbery. The interior of the tumulus was used as an [[air raid shelter]] during [[World War II]] and was visited by large numbers of people during the post-war archeology boom, causing much damage to the preservations of the murals.
The tumulus is about a ten-minute walk from [[Ukiha Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kyūdai Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The {{nihongo|'''Kusumyō-Shigesada Kofun'''|楠名・重定古墳}} is a pair of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Asada neighborhood of the city of [[Ukiha, Fukuoka|Ukiha]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus pair was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1922.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/173205|title=楠名・重定古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kusumyō-Shigesada Kofun are part of the Asada Kofun Cluster, located at the tip of the [[alluvial fan]] on the south bank of the [[Chikugo River]], at the foot of Mt. Minou. This area has many [[decorated kofun]], including the [[Tsukahanazuka Kofun]].<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Kusumyō Kofun is a two-tiered {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳||}}-style circular tomb with a diameter of approximately 32 meters and a height of 6 meters. The [[burial chamber]] is a horizontal-entry cave-style stone chamber that opens to the south, with the front chamber larger than the back chamber, and is thought to have been built in the early 7th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Shigesada Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has total length of approximately 51 meters, with a posterior circular portion diameter of approximately 44 meters, and height of 8.5 meters. it is believed to have been built in the latter half of the 6th century. The anterior portion has largely been lost, but the total length at the time of construction is estimated to be about 80 meters.The interior is a horizontal-entry stone burial chamber that opens almost to the south, with a total length of 17 meters made of large [[andesite]] monoliths. It is connected to the outside by a six meter long corridor. Decorative murals are on the walls, and although they have faded now, they use two colors, red and green, to create geometric patterns such as concentric circular patterns and [[bracken fern|''warabi'' fern]] patterns, as well as archery patterns depicting fletching, wristguard and quivers. The pattern composition is different between the rear chamber and the front chamber. The [[Edo period]] ''[[kokugaku]]'' scholar [[Hirata Atsutane]] interpreted this pattern as an ancient script, which he named {{nihongo|''Tsukushi moji''|筑紫文字|}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Historical records indicate that a large amount of [[grave goods]] could be found in this tumulus, but all have been lost to robbery. The interior of the tumulus was used as an [[air raid shelter]] during [[World War II]] and was visited by large numbers of people during the post-war archeology boom, causing much damage to the preservations of the murals.
The tumulus is about a ten-minute walk from [[Ukiha Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kyūdai Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The {{nihongo|'''Kusumyō-Shigesada Kofun'''|楠名・重定古墳}} is a pair of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Asada neighborhood of the city of [[Ukiha, Fukuoka|Ukiha]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus pair was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1922.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/173205|title=楠名・重定古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kusumyō-Shigesada Kofun are part of the Asada Kofun Cluster, located at the tip of the [[alluvial fan]] on the south bank of the [[Chikugo River]], at the foot of Mt. Minou. This area has many [[decorated kofun]], including the [[Tsukahanazuka Kofun]].<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

The [[burial chamber]] is a horizontal-entry cave-style stone chamber that opens to the south, with the front chamber larger than the back chamber, and is thought to have been built in the early 7th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Shigesada Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has total length of approximately 51 meters, with a posterior circular portion diameter of approximately 44 meters, and height of 8.5 meters. it is believed to have been built in the latter half of the 6th century. The anterior portion has largely been lost, but the total length at the time of construction is estimated to be about 80 meters.The interior is a horizontal-entry stone burial chamber that opens almost to the south, with a total length of 17 meters made of large [[andesite]] monoliths. It is connected to the outside by a six meter long corridor. The [[Edo period]] ''[[kokugaku]]'' scholar [[Hirata Atsutane]] interpreted this pattern as an ancient script, which he named {{nihongo|''Tsukushi moji''|筑紫文字|}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Historical records indicate that a large amount of [[grave goods]] could be found in this tumulus, but all have been lost to robbery. The interior of the tumulus was used as an [[air raid shelter]] during [[World War II]] and was visited by large numbers of people during the post-war archeology boom, causing much damage to the preservations of the murals.

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Kusumyō-Shigesada Kofun | native_name =楠名・重定古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Shigesada Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Shigesada Kofun sign | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kusumyō-Shigesada Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|19|57|N|130|47|40|E|region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ukiha, Fukuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th to 7th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|19|57|N|130|47|40|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kusumyō-Shigesada Kofun'''|楠名・重定古墳}} is a pair of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Asada neighborhood of the city of [[Ukiha, Fukuoka|Ukiha]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus pair was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1922.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/173205|title=楠名・重定古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kusumyō-Shigesada Kofun are part of the Asada Kofun Cluster, located at the tip of the [[alluvial fan]] on the south bank of the [[Chikugo River]], at the foot of Mt. Minou. This area has many [[decorated kofun]], including the [[Tsukahanazuka Kofun]].<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Kusumyō Kofun is a two-tiered {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳||}}-style circular tomb with a diameter of approximately 32 meters and a height of 6 meters. The [[burial chamber]] is a horizontal-entry cave-style stone chamber that opens to the south, with the front chamber larger than the back chamber, and is thought to have been built in the early 7th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Shigesada Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has total length of approximately 51 meters, with a posterior circular portion diameter of approximately 44 meters, and height of 8.5 meters. it is believed to have been built in the latter half of the 6th century. The anterior portion has largely been lost, but the total length at the time of construction is estimated to be about 80 meters.The interior is a horizontal-entry stone burial chamber that opens almost to the south, with a total length of 17 meters made of large [[andesite]] monoliths. It is connected to the outside by a six meter long corridor. Decorative murals are on the walls, and although they have faded now, they use two colors, red and green, to create geometric patterns such as concentric circular patterns and [[bracken fern|''warabi'' fern]] patterns, as well as archery patterns depicting fletching, wristguard and quivers. The pattern composition is different between the rear chamber and the front chamber. The [[Edo period]] ''[[kokugaku]]'' scholar [[Hirata Atsutane]] interpreted this pattern as an ancient script, which he named {{nihongo|''Tsukushi moji''|筑紫文字|}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Historical records indicate that a large amount of [[grave goods]] could be found in this tumulus, but all have been lost to robbery. The interior of the tumulus was used as an [[air raid shelter]] during [[World War II]] and was visited by large numbers of people during the post-war archeology boom, causing much damage to the preservations of the murals.
The tumulus is about a ten-minute walk from [[Ukiha Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kyūdai Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Kusumyō-Shigesada Kofun | native_name =楠名・重定古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Shigesada Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Shigesada Kofun sign | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kusumyō-Shigesada Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|19|57|N|130|47|40|E|region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ukiha, Fukuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th to 7th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|19|57|N|130|47|40|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kusumyō-Shigesada Kofun'''|楠名・重定古墳}} is a pair of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Asada neighborhood of the city of [[Ukiha, Fukuoka|Ukiha]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus pair was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1922.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/173205|title=楠名・重定古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kusumyō-Shigesada Kofun are part of the Asada Kofun Cluster, located at the tip of the [[alluvial fan]] on the south bank of the [[Chikugo River]], at the foot of Mt. Minou. This area has many [[decorated kofun]], including the [[Tsukahanazuka Kofun]].<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Kusumyō Kofun is a two-tiered {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳||}}-style circular tomb with a diameter of approximately 32 meters and a height of 6 meters. The [[burial chamber]] is a horizontal-entry cave-style stone chamber that opens to the south, with the front chamber larger than the back chamber, and is thought to have been built in the early 7th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Shigesada Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has total length of approximately 51 meters, with a posterior circular portion diameter of approximately 44 meters, and height of 8.5 meters. it is believed to have been built in the latter half of the 6th century. The anterior portion has largely been lost, but the total length at the time of construction is estimated to be about 80 meters.The interior is a horizontal-entry stone burial chamber that opens almost to the south, with a total length of 17 meters made of large [[andesite]] monoliths. It is connected to the outside by a six meter long corridor. Decorative murals are on the walls, and although they have faded now, they use two colors, red and green, to create geometric patterns such as concentric circular patterns and [[bracken fern|''warabi'' fern]] patterns, as well as archery patterns depicting fletching, wristguard and quivers. The pattern composition is different between the rear chamber and the front chamber. The [[Edo period]] ''[[kokugaku]]'' scholar [[Hirata Atsutane]] interpreted this pattern as an ancient script, which he named {{nihongo|''Tsukushi moji''|筑紫文字|}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Historical records indicate that a large amount of [[grave goods]] could be found in this tumulus, but all have been lost to robbery. The interior of the tumulus was used as an [[air raid shelter]] during [[World War II]] and was visited by large numbers of people during the post-war archeology boom, causing much damage to the preservations of the murals.
The tumulus is about a ten-minute walk from [[Ukiha Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kyūdai Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Kusumyō-Shigesada Kofun | native_name =楠名・重定古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Shigesada Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Shigesada Kofun sign | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kusumyō-Shigesada Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|19|57|N|130|47|40|E|region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ukiha, Fukuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th to 7th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|19|57|N|130|47|40|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kusumyō-Shigesada Kofun'''|楠名・重定古墳}} is a pair of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Asada neighborhood of the city of [[Ukiha, Fukuoka|Ukiha]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus pair was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1922.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/173205|title=楠名・重定古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kusumyō-Shigesada Kofun are part of the Asada Kofun Cluster, located at the tip of the [[alluvial fan]] on the south bank of the [[Chikugo River]], at the foot of Mt. Minou. This area has many [[decorated kofun]], including the [[Tsukahanazuka Kofun]].<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Kusumyō Kofun is a two-tiered {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}}-style circular tomb with a diameter of approximately 32 meters and a height of 6 meters. The [[burial chamber]] is a horizontal-entry cave-style stone chamber that opens to the south, with the front chamber larger than the back chamber, and is thought to have been built in the early 7th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Shigesada Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has total length of approximately 51 meters, with a posterior circular portion diameter of approximately 44 meters, and height of 8.5 meters. it is believed to have been built in the latter half of the 6th century. The anterior portion has largely been lost, but the total length at the time of construction is estimated to be about 80 meters.The interior is a horizontal-entry stone burial chamber that opens almost to the south, with a total length of 17 meters made of large [[andesite]] monoliths. It is connected to the outside by a six meter long corridor. Decorative murals are on the walls, and although they have faded now, they use two colors, red and green, to create geometric patterns such as concentric circular patterns and [[bracken fern|''warabi'' fern]] patterns, as well as archery patterns depicting fletching, wristguard and quivers. The pattern composition is different between the rear chamber and the front chamber. The [[Edo period]] ''[[kokugaku]]'' scholar [[Hirata Atsutane]] interpreted this pattern as an ancient script, which he named {{nihongo|''Tsukushi moji''|筑紫文字|}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Historical records indicate that a large amount of [[grave goods]] could be found in this tumulus, but all have been lost to robbery. The interior of the tumulus was used as an [[air raid shelter]] during [[World War II]] and was visited by large numbers of people during the post-war archeology boom, causing much damage to the preservations of the murals.
The tumulus is about a ten-minute walk from [[Ukiha Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kyūdai Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Kusumyō-Shigesada Kofun | native_name =楠名・重定古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Shigesada Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Shigesada Kofun sign | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kusumyō-Shigesada Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|19|57|N|130|47|40|E|region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ukiha, Fukuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th to 7th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|19|57|N|130|47|40|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kusumyō-Shigesada Kofun'''|楠名・重定古墳}} is a pair of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Asada neighborhood of the city of [[Ukiha, Fukuoka|Ukiha]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus pair was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1922.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/173205|title=楠名・重定古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kusumyō-Shigesada Kofun are part of the Asada Kofun Cluster, located at the tip of the [[alluvial fan]] on the south bank of the [[Chikugo River]], at the foot of Mt. Minou. This area has many [[decorated kofun]], including the [[Tsukahanazuka Kofun]].<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Kusumyō Kofun is a two-tiered {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}}-style circular tomb with a diameter of approximately 32 meters and a height of 6 meters. The [[burial chamber]] is a horizontal-entry cave-style stone chamber that opens to the south, with the front chamber larger than the back chamber, and is thought to have been built in the early 7th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Shigesada Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has total length of approximately 51 meters, with a posterior circular portion diameter of approximately 44 meters, and height of 8.5 meters. it is believed to have been built in the latter half of the 6th century. The anterior portion has largely been lost, but the total length at the time of construction is estimated to be about 80 meters.The interior is a horizontal-entry stone burial chamber that opens almost to the south, with a total length of 17 meters made of large [[andesite]] monoliths. It is connected to the outside by a six meter long corridor. Decorative murals are on the walls, and although they have faded now, they use two colors, red and green, to create geometric patterns such as concentric circular patterns and [[bracken fern|''warabi'' fern]] patterns, as well as archery patterns depicting fletching, wristguard and quivers. The pattern composition is different between the rear chamber and the front chamber. The [[Edo period]] ''[[kokugaku]]'' scholar [[Hirata Atsutane]] interpreted this pattern as an ancient script, which he named {{nihongo|''Tsukushi moji''|筑紫文字|}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Historical records indicate that a large amount of [[grave goods]] could be found in this tumulus, but all have been lost to robbery. The interior of the tumulus was used as an [[air raid shelter]] during [[World War II]] and was visited by large numbers of people during the post-war archeology boom, causing much damage to the preservations of the murals.
The tumulus is about a ten-minute walk from [[Ukiha Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kyūdai Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Kusumyō-Shigesada Kofun | native_name =楠名・重定古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Shigesada Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Shigesada Kofun sign | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kusumyō-Shigesada Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|19|57|N|130|47|40|E|region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ukiha, Fukuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th to 7th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|19|57|N|130|47|40|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kusumyō-Shigesada Kofun'''|楠名・重定古墳}} is a pair of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Asada neighborhood of the city of [[Ukiha, Fukuoka|Ukiha]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus pair was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1922.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/173205|title=楠名・重定古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kusumyō-Shigesada Kofun are part of the Asada Kofun Cluster, located at the tip of the [[alluvial fan]] on the south bank of the [[Chikugo River]], at the foot of Mt. Minou. This area has many [[decorated kofun]], including the [[Tsukahanazuka Kofun]].<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Kusumyō Kofun is a two-tiered {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}}-style circular tomb with a diameter of approximately 32 meters and a height of 6 meters. The [[burial chamber]] is a horizontal-entry cave-style stone chamber that opens to the south, with the front chamber larger than the back chamber, and is thought to have been built in the early 7th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Shigesada Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has total length of approximately 51 meters, with a posterior circular portion diameter of approximately 44 meters, and height of 8.5 meters. it is believed to have been built in the latter half of the 6th century. The anterior portion has largely been lost, but the total length at the time of construction is estimated to be about 80 meters.The interior is a horizontal-entry stone burial chamber that opens almost to the south, with a total length of 17 meters made of large [[andesite]] monoliths. It is connected to the outside by a six meter long corridor. Decorative murals are on the walls, and although they have faded now, they use two colors, red and green, to create geometric patterns such as concentric circular patterns and [[bracken fern|''warabi'' fern]] patterns, as well as archery patterns depicting fletching, wristguard and quivers. The pattern composition is different between the rear chamber and the front chamber. The [[Edo period]] ''[[kokugaku]]'' scholar [[Hirata Atsutane]] interpreted this pattern as an ancient script, which he named {{nihongo|''Tsukushi moji''|筑紫文字|}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Historical records indicate that a large amount of [[grave goods]] could be found in this tumulus, but all have been lost to robbery. The interior of the tumulus was used as an [[air raid shelter]] during [[World War II]] and was visited by large numbers of people during the post-war archeology boom, causing much damage to the preservations of the murals.
The tumulus is about a ten-minute walk from [[Ukiha Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kyūdai Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Kusumyō-Shigesada Kofun | native_name =楠名・重定古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Shigesada Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Shigesada Kofun sign | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kusumyō-Shigesada Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|19|57|N|130|47|40|E|region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ukiha, Fukuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th to 7th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|19|57|N|130|47|40|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kusumyō-Shigesada Kofun'''|楠名・重定古墳}} is a pair of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Asada neighborhood of the city of [[Ukiha, Fukuoka|Ukiha]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus pair was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1922.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/173205|title=楠名・重定古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kusumyō-Shigesada Kofun are part of the Asada Kofun Cluster, located at the tip of the [[alluvial fan]] on the south bank of the [[Chikugo River]], at the foot of Mt. Minou. This area has many [[decorated kofun]], including the [[Tsukahanazuka Kofun]].<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Kusumyō Kofun is a two-tiered {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}}-style circular tomb with a diameter of approximately 32 meters and a height of 6 meters. The [[burial chamber]] is a horizontal-entry cave-style stone chamber that opens to the south, with the front chamber larger than the back chamber, and is thought to have been built in the early 7th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Shigesada Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has total length of approximately 51 meters, with a posterior circular portion diameter of approximately 44 meters, and height of 8.5 meters. it is believed to have been built in the latter half of the 6th century. The anterior portion has largely been lost, but the total length at the time of construction is estimated to be about 80 meters.The interior is a horizontal-entry stone burial chamber that opens almost to the south, with a total length of 17 meters made of large [[andesite]] monoliths. It is connected to the outside by a six meter long corridor. Decorative murals are on the walls, and although they have faded now, they use two colors, red and green, to create geometric patterns such as concentric circular patterns and [[bracken fern|''warabi'' fern]] patterns, as well as archery patterns depicting fletching, wristguard and quivers. The pattern composition is different between the rear chamber and the front chamber. The [[Edo period]] ''[[kokugaku]]'' scholar [[Hirata Atsutane]] interpreted this pattern as an ancient script, which he named {{nihongo|''Tsukushi moji''|筑紫文字|}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Historical records indicate that a large amount of [[grave goods]] could be found in this tumulus, but all have been lost to robbery. The interior of the tumulus was used as an [[air raid shelter]] during [[World War II]] and was visited by large numbers of people during the post-war archeology boom, causing much damage to the preservations of the murals.
The tumulus is about a ten-minute walk from [[Ukiha Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kyūdai Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]

==Overview== The Kusumyō-Shigesada Kofun are part of the Asada Kofun Cluster, located at the tip of the [[alluvial fan]] on the south bank of the [[Chikugo River]], at the foot of Mt. Minou. This area has many [[decorated kofun]], including the [[Tsukahanazuka Kofun]].<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Kusumyō Kofun is a two-tiered {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}}-style circular tomb with a diameter of approximately 32 meters and a height of 6 meters. The [[burial chamber]] is a horizontal-entry cave-style stone chamber that opens to the south, with the front chamber larger than the back chamber, and is thought to have been built in the early 7th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Shigesada Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has total length of approximately 51 meters, with a posterior circular portion diameter of approximately 44 meters, and height of 8.5 meters. it is believed to have been built in the latter half of the 6th century. The anterior portion has largely been lost, but the total length at the time of construction is estimated to be about 80 meters.The interior is a horizontal-entry stone burial chamber that opens almost to the south, with a total length of 17 meters made of large [[andesite]] monoliths. It is connected to the outside by a six meter long corridor. Decorative murals are on the walls, and although they have faded now, they use two colors, red and green, to create geometric patterns such as concentric circular patterns and [[bracken fern|''warabi'' fern]] patterns, as well as archery patterns depicting fletching, wristguard and quivers. The pattern composition is different between the rear chamber and the front chamber. The [[Edo period]] ''[[kokugaku]]'' scholar [[Hirata Atsutane]] interpreted this pattern as an ancient script, which he named {{nihongo|''Tsukushi moji''|筑紫文字|}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Historical records indicate that a large amount of [[grave goods]] could be found in this tumulus, but all have been lost to robbery. The interior of the tumulus was used as an [[air raid shelter]] during [[World War II]] and was visited by large numbers of people during the post-war archeology boom, causing much damage to the preservations of the murals.
The tumulus is about a ten-minute walk from [[Ukiha Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kyūdai Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The {{nihongo|'''Kusumyō-Shigesada Kofun'''|楠名・重定古墳}} is a pair of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Asada neighborhood of the city of [[Ukiha, Fukuoka|Ukiha]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus pair was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1922.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/173205|title=楠名・重定古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kusumyō-Shigesada Kofun are part of the Asada Kofun Cluster, located at the tip of the [[alluvial fan]] on the south bank of the [[Chikugo River]], at the foot of Mt. Minou. This area has many [[decorated kofun]], including the [[Tsukahanazuka Kofun]].<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

The [[burial chamber]] is a horizontal-entry cave-style stone chamber that opens to the south, with the front chamber larger than the back chamber, and is thought to have been built in the early 7th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Shigesada Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has total length of approximately 51 meters, with a posterior circular portion diameter of approximately 44 meters, and height of 8.5 meters. it is believed to have been built in the latter half of the 6th century. The anterior portion has largely been lost, but the total length at the time of construction is estimated to be about 80 meters.The interior is a horizontal-entry stone burial chamber that opens almost to the south, with a total length of 17 meters made of large [[andesite]] monoliths. It is connected to the outside by a six meter long corridor. Decorative murals are on the walls, and although they have faded now, they use two colors, red and green, to create geometric patterns such as concentric circular patterns and [[bracken fern|''warabi'' fern]] patterns, as well as archery patterns depicting fletching, wristguard and quivers. The pattern composition is different between the rear chamber and the front chamber. The [[Edo period]] ''[[kokugaku]]'' scholar [[Hirata Atsutane]] interpreted this pattern as an ancient script, which he named {{nihongo|''Tsukushi moji''|筑紫文字|}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Historical records indicate that a large amount of [[grave goods]] could be found in this tumulus, but all have been lost to robbery. The interior of the tumulus was used as an [[air raid shelter]] during [[World War II]] and was visited by large numbers of people during the post-war archeology boom, causing much damage to the preservations of the murals.

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Kusumyō-Shigesada Kofun | native_name =楠名・重定古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Shigesada Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Shigesada Kofun sign | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kusumyō-Shigesada Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|19|57|N|130|47|40|E|region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ukiha, Fukuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th to 7th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|19|57|N|130|47|40|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kusumyō-Shigesada Kofun'''|楠名・重定古墳}} is a pair of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Asada neighborhood of the city of [[Ukiha, Fukuoka|Ukiha]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus pair was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1922.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/173205|title=楠名・重定古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kusumyō-Shigesada Kofun are part of the Asada Kofun Cluster, located at the tip of the [[alluvial fan]] on the south bank of the [[Chikugo River]], at the foot of Mt. Minou. This area has many [[decorated kofun]], including the [[Tsukahanazuka Kofun]].<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Kusumyō Kofun is a two-tiered {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}}-style circular tomb with a diameter of approximately 32 meters and a height of 6 meters. The [[burial chamber]] is a horizontal-entry cave-style stone chamber that opens to the south, with the front chamber larger than the back chamber, and is thought to have been built in the early 7th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Shigesada Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has total length of approximately 51 meters, with a posterior circular portion diameter of approximately 44 meters, and height of 8.5 meters. it is believed to have been built in the latter half of the 6th century. The anterior portion has largely been lost, but the total length at the time of construction is estimated to be about 80 meters.The interior is a horizontal-entry stone burial chamber that opens almost to the south, with a total length of 17 meters made of large [[andesite]] monoliths. It is connected to the outside by a six meter long corridor. Decorative murals are on the walls, and although they have faded now, they use two colors, red and green, to create geometric patterns such as concentric circular patterns and [[bracken fern|''warabi'' fern]] patterns, as well as archery patterns depicting fletching, wristguard and quivers. The pattern composition is different between the rear chamber and the front chamber. The [[Edo period]] ''[[kokugaku]]'' scholar [[Hirata Atsutane]] interpreted this pattern as an ancient script, which he named {{nihongo|''Tsukushi moji''|筑紫文字|}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Historical records indicate that a large amount of [[grave goods]] could be found in this tumulus, but all have been lost to robbery. The interior of the tumulus was used as an [[air raid shelter]] during [[World War II]] and was visited by large numbers of people during the post-war archeology boom, causing much damage to the preservations of the murals.
The tumulus is about a ten-minute walk from [[Ukiha Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kyūdai Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Kusumyō-Shigesada Kofun | native_name =楠名・重定古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Shigesada Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Shigesada Kofun sign | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kusumyō-Shigesada Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|19|57|N|130|47|40|E|region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ukiha, Fukuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th to 7th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|19|57|N|130|47|40|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kusumyō-Shigesada Kofun'''|楠名・重定古墳}} is a pair of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Asada neighborhood of the city of [[Ukiha, Fukuoka|Ukiha]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus pair was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1922.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/173205|title=楠名・重定古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kusumyō-Shigesada Kofun are part of the Asada Kofun Cluster, located at the tip of the [[alluvial fan]] on the south bank of the [[Chikugo River]], at the foot of Mt. Minou. This area has many [[decorated kofun]], including the [[Tsukahanazuka Kofun]].<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Kusumyō Kofun is a two-tiered {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}}-style circular tomb with a diameter of approximately 32 meters and a height of 6 meters. The [[burial chamber]] is a horizontal-entry cave-style stone chamber that opens to the south, with the front chamber larger than the back chamber, and is thought to have been built in the early 7th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Shigesada Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has total length of approximately 51 meters, with a posterior circular portion diameter of approximately 44 meters, and height of 8.5 meters. it is believed to have been built in the latter half of the 6th century. The anterior portion has largely been lost, but the total length at the time of construction is estimated to be about 80 meters.The interior is a horizontal-entry stone burial chamber that opens almost to the south, with a total length of 17 meters made of large [[andesite]] monoliths. It is connected to the outside by a six meter long corridor. Decorative murals are on the walls, and although they have faded now, they use two colors, red and green, to create geometric patterns such as concentric circular patterns and [[bracken fern|''warabi'' fern]] patterns, as well as archery patterns depicting fletching, wristguard and quivers. The pattern composition is different between the rear chamber and the front chamber. The [[Edo period]] ''[[kokugaku]]'' scholar [[Hirata Atsutane]] interpreted this pattern as an ancient script, which he named {{nihongo|''Tsukushi moji''|筑紫文字|}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Historical records indicate that a large amount of [[grave goods]] could be found in this tumulus, but all have been lost to robbery. The interior of the tumulus was used as an [[air raid shelter]] during [[World War II]] and was visited by large numbers of people during the post-war archeology boom, causing much damage to the preservations of the murals.
The tumulus is about a ten-minute walk from [[Ukiha Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kyūdai Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://ukihalove.jp/contents/kusumyoukofun/ Ukiha tourism home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/spot/14866 Fukuoka Tourism web] {{in lang|ja}}

The anterior portion has largely been lost, but the total length at the time of construction is estimated to be about 80 meters.The interior is a horizontal-entry stone burial chamber that opens almost to the south, with a total length of 17 meters made of large [[andesite]] monoliths. It is connected to the outside by a six meter long corridor. Decorative murals are on the walls, and although they have faded now, they use two colors, red and green, to create geometric patterns such as concentric circular patterns and [[bracken fern|''warabi'' fern]] patterns, as well as archery patterns depicting fletching, wristguard and quivers. The pattern composition is different between the rear chamber and the front chamber. The [[Edo period]] ''[[kokugaku]]'' scholar [[Hirata Atsutane]] interpreted this pattern as an ancient script, which he named {{nihongo|''Tsukushi moji''|筑紫文字|}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Historical records indicate that a large amount of [[grave goods]] could be found in this tumulus, but all have been lost to robbery. The interior of the tumulus was used as an [[air raid shelter]] during [[World War II]] and was visited by large numbers of people during the post-war archeology boom, causing much damage to the preservations of the murals.
The tumulus is about a ten-minute walk from [[Ukiha Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kyūdai Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The {{nihongo|'''Kusumyō-Shigesada Kofun'''|楠名・重定古墳}} is a pair of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Asada neighborhood of the city of [[Ukiha, Fukuoka|Ukiha]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus pair was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1922.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/173205|title=楠名・重定古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kusumyō-Shigesada Kofun are part of the Asada Kofun Cluster, located at the tip of the [[alluvial fan]] on the south bank of the [[Chikugo River]], at the foot of Mt. Minou. This area has many [[decorated kofun]], including the [[Tsukahanazuka Kofun]].<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Kusumyō Kofun is a two-tiered {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}}-style circular tomb with a diameter of approximately 32 meters and a height of 6 meters. The [[burial chamber]] is a horizontal-entry cave-style stone chamber that opens to the south, with the front chamber larger than the back chamber, and is thought to have been built in the early 7th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Shigesada Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has total length of approximately 51 meters, with a posterior circular portion diameter of approximately 44 meters, and height of 8.5 meters. it is believed to have been built in the latter half of the 6th century. The anterior portion has largely been lost, but the total length at the time of construction is estimated to be about 80 meters.The interior is a horizontal-entry stone burial chamber that opens almost to the south, with a total length of 17 meters made of large [[andesite]] monoliths. It is connected to the outside by a six meter long corridor. Decorative murals are on the walls, and although they have faded now, they use two colors, red and green, to create geometric patterns such as concentric circular patterns and [[bracken fern|''warabi'' fern]] patterns, as well as archery patterns depicting fletching, wristguard and quivers. The pattern composition is different between the rear chamber and the front chamber. The [[Edo period]] ''[[kokugaku]]'' scholar [[Hirata Atsutane]] interpreted this pattern as an ancient script, which he named {{nihongo|''Tsukushi moji''|筑紫文字|}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Historical records indicate that a large amount of [[grave goods]] could be found in this tumulus, but all have been lost to robbery. The interior of the tumulus was used as an [[air raid shelter]] during [[World War II]] and was visited by large numbers of people during the post-war archeology boom, causing much damage to the preservations of the murals.
The tumulus is about a ten-minute walk from [[Ukiha Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kyūdai Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Kusumyō-Shigesada Kofun | native_name =楠名・重定古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Shigesada Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Shigesada Kofun sign | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kusumyō-Shigesada Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|19|57|N|130|47|40|E|region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Ukiha, Fukuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th to 7th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|19|57|N|130|47|40|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kusumyō-Shigesada Kofun'''|楠名・重定古墳}} is a pair of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Asada neighborhood of the city of [[Ukiha, Fukuoka|Ukiha]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus pair was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1922.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/173205|title=楠名・重定古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kusumyō-Shigesada Kofun are part of the Asada Kofun Cluster, located at the tip of the [[alluvial fan]] on the south bank of the [[Chikugo River]], at the foot of Mt. Minou. This area has many [[decorated kofun]], including the [[Tsukahanazuka Kofun]].<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The Kusumyō Kofun is a two-tiered {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}}-style circular tomb with a diameter of approximately 32 meters and a height of 6 meters. The [[burial chamber]] is a horizontal-entry cave-style stone chamber that opens to the south, with the front chamber larger than the back chamber, and is thought to have been built in the early 7th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Shigesada Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has total length of approximately 51 meters, with a posterior circular portion diameter of approximately 44 meters, and height of 8.5 meters. it is believed to have been built in the latter half of the 6th century. The anterior portion has largely been lost, but the total length at the time of construction is estimated to be about 80 meters.The interior is a horizontal-entry stone burial chamber that opens almost to the south, with a total length of 17 meters made of large [[andesite]] monoliths. It is connected to the outside by a six meter long corridor. Decorative murals are on the walls, and although they have faded now, they use two colors, red and green, to create geometric patterns such as concentric circular patterns and [[bracken fern|''warabi'' fern]] patterns, as well as archery patterns depicting fletching, wristguard and quivers. The pattern composition is different between the rear chamber and the front chamber. The [[Edo period]] ''[[kokugaku]]'' scholar [[Hirata Atsutane]] interpreted this pattern as an ancient script, which he named {{nihongo|''Tsukushi moji''|筑紫文字|}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Historical records indicate that a large amount of [[grave goods]] could be found in this tumulus, but all have been lost to robbery. The interior of the tumulus was used as an [[air raid shelter]] during [[World War II]] and was visited by large numbers of people during the post-war archeology boom, causing much damage to the preservations of the murals.
The tumulus is about a ten-minute walk from [[Ukiha Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kyūdai Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]

The Shigesada Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has total length of approximately 51 meters, with a posterior circular portion diameter of approximately 44 meters, and height of 8.5 meters. it is believed to have been built in the latter half of the 6th century. The anterior portion has largely been lost, but the total length at the time of construction is estimated to be about 80 meters.The interior is a horizontal-entry stone burial chamber that opens almost to the south, with a total length of 17 meters made of large [[andesite]] monoliths. It is connected to the outside by a six meter long corridor. Decorative murals are on the walls, and although they have faded now, they use two colors, red and green, to create geometric patterns such as concentric circular patterns and [[bracken fern|''warabi'' fern]] patterns, as well as archery patterns depicting fletching, wristguard and quivers. The pattern composition is different between the rear chamber and the front chamber. The [[Edo period]] ''[[kokugaku]]'' scholar [[Hirata Atsutane]] interpreted this pattern as an ancient script, which he named {{nihongo|''Tsukushi moji''|筑紫文字|}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Historical records indicate that a large amount of [[grave goods]] could be found in this tumulus, but all have been lost to robbery. The interior of the tumulus was used as an [[air raid shelter]] during [[World War II]] and was visited by large numbers of people during the post-war archeology boom, causing much damage to the preservations of the murals.
The tumulus is about a ten-minute walk from [[Ukiha Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kyūdai Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The {{nihongo|'''Kusumyō-Shigesada Kofun'''|楠名・重定古墳}} is a pair of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Asada neighborhood of the city of [[Ukiha, Fukuoka|Ukiha]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus pair was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1922.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/173205|title=楠名・重定古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Kusumyō-Shigesada Kofun are part of the Asada Kofun Cluster, located at the tip of the [[alluvial fan]] on the south bank of the [[Chikugo River]], at the foot of Mt. Minou. This area has many [[decorated kofun]], including the [[Tsukahanazuka Kofun]].<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

The [[burial chamber]] is a horizontal-entry cave-style stone chamber that opens to the south, with the front chamber larger than the back chamber, and is thought to have been built in the early 7th century.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Shigesada Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has total length of approximately 51 meters, with a posterior circular portion diameter of approximately 44 meters, and height of 8.5 meters. it is believed to have been built in the latter half of the 6th century. The anterior portion has largely been lost, but the total length at the time of construction is estimated to be about 80 meters.The interior is a horizontal-entry stone burial chamber that opens almost to the south, with a total length of 17 meters made of large [[andesite]] monoliths. It is connected to the outside by a six meter long corridor. Decorative murals are on the walls, and although they have faded now, they use two colors, red and green, to create geometric patterns such as concentric circular patterns and [[bracken fern|''warabi'' fern]] patterns, as well as archery patterns depicting fletching, wristguard and quivers. The pattern composition is different between the rear chamber and the front chamber. The [[Edo period]] ''[[kokugaku]]'' scholar [[Hirata Atsutane]] interpreted this pattern as an ancient script, which he named {{nihongo|''Tsukushi moji''|筑紫文字|}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Historical records indicate that a large amount of [[grave goods]] could be found in this tumulus, but all have been lost to robbery. The interior of the tumulus was used as an [[air raid shelter]] during [[World War II]] and was visited by large numbers of people during the post-war archeology boom, causing much damage to the preservations of the murals.
The tumulus is about a ten-minute walk from [[Ukiha Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kyūdai Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The tumulus is about a ten-minute walk from [[Ukiha Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kyūdai Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
